questions.question: Consider the statement ¬∃x (P(x) ∧ Q(x)). What does this statement mean in terms of the properties P and Q?
questions.hint: Hint to solve the question: Think about the meaning of existential quantification (∃) and negation (¬)

questions.question: Simplify the expression ∀x∈A (P(x) → Q(x)). What is the intended meaning of this expression?
questions.hint: Hint to solve the question: Think about the meaning of universal quantification (∀) and material implication (→)

questions.question: Consider the statement ∃x∈A (P(x) ∧ Q(x)). If A = {1, 2, 3}, P(x) = x > 2, and Q(x) = x < 3, what is the truth value of this statement?
questions.hint: Hint to solve the question: Think about the meaning of existential quantification (∃) and the given properties P and Q
